The '''living quarters''' of [[Jedi High Council/Legends|High Council]] member [[Shaak Ti/Legends|Shaak Ti]] served her from her appointment to the High Council until the dissolution of the Order during the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]].

==History==
These quarters were assigned to Master Shaak Ti upon her appointment to the High Council following the [[Death/Legends|death]] of Master [[Yaddle/Legends|Yaddle]]. Within these chambers, Ti maintained an atmosphere conducive for [[Meditation/Legends|meditation]] on [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="J:ST" />

During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Master Ti was distraught after the [[Battle of Brentaal IV (Clone Wars)|Battle of Brentaal IV]] and sought meditation to clear her head. While deep in meditation, fellow Master [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] joined her and encouraged Ti to sit and meditate with him, not as warriors or as [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], but as friends.<ref name="J:ST" />

[[19 BBY/Legends|Two years later]], Ti was stationed at the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] while Master [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] went to [[Showdown on Coruscant|arrest]] the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]. Going to her chambers to meditate, she sensed Windu's death and began to rally all the Jedi in the Temple. As the Temple was [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|seized]] by the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]], Master Ti confronted the newly christened [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] in the halls as Jedi fell around her. Fighting to a stalemate, Ti managed to end the duel and gather a group of [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Knights]] and evacuate the Temple.<ref name="Star Wars: The Force Unleashed">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''</ref>

==Layout==
Located on the Council Members' Level of the [[High Council Tower/Legends|High Council Tower]], the room was accessed through a black door off a hallway that ringed the tower's central shaft. Bare and hexagonal, the room was empty save a few metallic meditation pads and a sleep couch. Like the rest of the Masters' accommodations, the chamber was ringed by pillars which framed blinded windows looking out onto the cityscape of [[Galactic City/Legends|Galactic City]].<ref name="J:ST" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:SHAAK_edited.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Shaak Ti in her quarters in a deleted scene from Episode III.]]

Shaak Ti's quarters were constructed as a set for [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Revenge of the Sith'']]. Though it was filmed, Ti's death scene never made it into the final release. Shaak Ti's chamber actually appeared two years prior to its scheduled debut in ''Revenge of the Sith'', it was featured at the end of the comic, ''[[Jedi: Shaak Ti]]''. Her quarters were also featured in the non-canononical version of her death (based on the original filmed version in which she dies in her quarters at the hands of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]) in [[LEGO Star Wars: The Video Game]].
